reviewing.work_completion: 6 of 13 entrypoint files refactored to canonical pattern; remaining 7 deferred to successor EA-4

guide_review_response.summary: Independent multi-file verification of EA-instance-3's claimed 2.4 partial work product (6 of 13 entrypoint files refactored). Per the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate and the GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST w12-w15 protocol, this Guide instance ran every claim through disk-level verification rather than trusting the EA's narrative. Result: every substantive claim in the EA completion report reproduces on disk. The single-commit discipline at ccdd82b holds (1 file changed, +291/-0, docs-only). The 6 refactored Python files match the canonical pattern by both direct read AND grep verification. Defaults preserve byte-equivalent BlarAI behavior: `_project_context.resolve(None, None)` returns `ProjectContext(root=C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI, vikunja_project_id=3, name='BlarAI')` and the overlay path resolves to the existing `tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml`. Helper-handling is consistent and correctly described: `REPO_ROOT` preserved in both daily_digest and weekly_summary; `build_digest` / `build_summary` accept `*, ctx: ProjectContext | None = None` with auto-resolve fallback; `_git_log_oneline_since` and `_git_log_stats` accept `repo_root: Path = REPO_ROOT` kwarg; `main()` threads `ctx=ctx` into the builders. Pytest tools/ baseline holds at 280 passed (matches v0e baseline from EA-iter-2 gate; zero regression). Fleet pause invariant holds (state.json `fleet_paused=true`, reason "platform separation v2 in progress", schema_version=1) and stash@{0} (`wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002`) is byte-identical to its pre-session state. All four decisions D1-D4 are RATIFIED. Four minor heads-up items are surfaced for the next EA (EA-4) below; none rise to a blocking-correction level for EA-3, which has already emitted and is closing this session.

recipe.command_run: git log --oneline -3 ; git show --stat ccdd82b ; git status --porcelain ; git diff --stat
recipe.observed:
git log -3 confirms ccdd82b is the EA-3 commit. Current HEAD has advanced to 9d17e7f (LA's between-instance Guide-instance-4-to-5 handoff brief, also docs-only) -- this is an EXPECTED LA-side commit between EA emission and Guide spawn, not an EA violation.
git show --stat ccdd82b output: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v3.xml | 291 +++++++++ 1 file changed, 291 insertions(+)
git diff --stat for the 6 refactored files (line counts vs EA prediction): tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py | 34 (predicted 34) MATCH tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py | 34 (predicted 34) MATCH tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py | 65 (predicted 65) MATCH tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py | 63 (predicted 63) MATCH tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py | 34 (predicted 34) MATCH tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py | 34 (predicted 34) MATCH
Plus the 4 inherited Stage-2 paths from v2 (pyproject.toml, config.yaml, config_loader.py, test_config_loader.py) all present as expected.
recipe.unexpected_extras_observed_and_classifie: FOUR additional working-tree entries NOT predicted by EA-3 in v3 handoff -- ALL classified as LA-side housekeeping post-emission, NOT EA violations: (a) M docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_PROMPT.xml (+19 lines) -- LA's update to v2.1 with file-output-discipline carve-out. Predates the EA but not committed when EA emitted; now visible because EA's v3 handoff captured working tree before LA's later edits. (b) D "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.10 blocked on two corrections - 01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT.xml" (c) D "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.7 re-verify - 01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT.xml" (b)+(c) are LA's directory-rename housekeeping (capital "Temp for responses" -> lowercase "temp_for_responses" per GUIDE_PROMPT v2.1 file-output convention). (d) ?? docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ -- the new lowercase directory holding the iteration-1/iteration-2 review files (which EA-3 v3-ground-truth listed under the old "Temp for responses" path). These are NOT EA-3 commits. They are LA-side workspace state that the next EA's pickup verification must expect.

recipe.command_run: foreach file in 6 refactored files: python -m py_compile $file
recipe.observed: OK x6 -- all 6 refactored files compile cleanly. Reproducible.

Direct read of all 6 files confirms the canonical pattern: `import argparse`, `from tools import _project_context`, `parser.add_argument("--project-root", type=str, ...)`, `parser.add_argument("--project-id", type=int, ...)`, `def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:`, `_project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id)`, and `config_loader.load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/.../f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml")`.
Grep for `_project_context.resolve(` returned 8 hits across the 6 entrypoints (exactly the expected 6 main() invocations + 2 auto-resolve fallbacks in build_digest/build_summary helpers, by design).
Grep for `f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml"` returned 7 files -- the 6 entrypoints + config_loader.py (the latter is the inherited Stage 2.3 _resolve_project_overlay_path helper). Correct.

recipe.command_run: python -c "from tools import _project_context; ctx = _project_context.resolve(None, None); print(repr(ctx))"
recipe.interpretation: Confirms EA's defaults_preservation attestation: ctx.name="BlarAI" -> ctx.name.lower()="blarai" -> blarai.yaml overlay file exists -> load_config() under no-flag invocation produces the same merged config as pre-2.3 (per the test_load_config_default_overlay_restores_blarai_path Stage 2.3 test, also passing in the 280-baseline run).

recipe.observed:
daily_digest: REPO_ROOT exists: True build_digest sig: (client: 'LiveVikunjaClient', *, ctx: '_project_context.ProjectContext | None' = None) -> 'str' _git_log_oneline_since sig: (since_iso: 'str', repo_root: 'Path' = WindowsPath('C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI')) -> 'list[str]'
weekly_summary: REPO_ROOT exists: True build_summary sig: (client: 'LiveVikunjaClient', *, ctx: '_project_context.ProjectContext | None' = None) -> 'tuple[str, str]' _git_log_stats sig: (since: 'datetime', until: 'datetime', repo_root: 'Path' = WindowsPath('C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI')) -> 'dict[str, int]'
recipe.interpretation: D1 (REPO_ROOT preservation) and D2 (helper ctx kwarg discipline) both verified on disk: signatures exactly match the EA's claims. dashboard_maintainer.py imports _git_log_oneline_since from daily_digest (line 53-56) and calls it without args, confirming the in-process caller D1 was protecting against -- removing REPO_ROOT would break that import-side caller. EA's design is sound.

recipe.observed:
git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002
tools/autonomy_budget/state.json: { "fleet_paused": true, "fleet_paused_reason": "platform separation v2 in progress", "last_updated_by": "copilot_agent", "last_updated_utc": "2026-04-24T20:46:12Z", "schema_version": 1, ... }
recipe.interpretation: Both invariants hold: stash byte-identical to pre-session state (per its 20260424_013002 timestamp); fleet_paused remains true with the original reason and the original last_updated_by="copilot_agent" timestamp. NO state.resume_fleet() was called this session. Acks n=2 and n=4 from the iteration-2 EA gate verbatim_carryforward both honored.

check.ea_claim: 6 of 13 entrypoint files refactored to canonical pattern.
check.independent_verification: Read all 6 files end-to-end. Each contains: - `import argparse` and `from tools import _project_context` at module top. - `_build_parser()` returning ArgumentParser with `--project-root` (type=str, default=None) and `--project-id` (type=int, default=None) flags. - `main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:` signature. - `ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id)` call. - `config_loader.load_config(path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", project_overlay_path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "projects" / f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml")` invocation. Argparse declarations are NECESSARY-AND-SUFFICIENT-COMBINED-WITH-resolve()-call (per dn14 sufficiency rule). Both verified for all 6 files.

check.ea_claim: Only ONE commit this session (ccdd82b), docs-only, +291/-0, file = v3 handoff XML.
check.independent_verification: git show --stat ccdd82b output: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v3.xml | 291 +++++++++ 1 file changed, 291 insertions(+) EXACTLY ONE file, EXACTLY 291 insertions, EXACTLY 0 deletions, EXACTLY the v3 handoff XML path. Refactor work is intentionally UNCOMMITTED in working tree (10 M + new untracked artifacts visible per V1 above). i1-4 and dn15 binding both honored. NOT a violation.

check.ea_claim: EA-3 has not yet reached item 2.5 (PS1 work).
check.independent_verification: Confirmed EA-3 stopped after Item 2.4 partial (6 of 13 files); Item 2.5 PS1 work is in the v3 handoff <work_remaining> for the successor. critical_notify.ps1 inspection rule from Guide-#4 iteration-2 approval (acks for the EA after iteration-2) carries forward to the successor EA-4 unchanged. No w14 violation by EA-3; this watchpoint properly recurs for EA-4.

check.ea_claim: v3 handoff defers tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py ctx-thread to Item 2.6: "For Item 2.4, NO ctx-related change (HTTP client has no project knowledge). Re-confirm at Item 2.6 whether the legacy client gets folded entirely or kept as a thin shim."
check.independent_verification:
Grep confirmed tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28 contains `cfg = config_loader.load_config()` (zero-arg). Today this works because load_config() with zero args invokes the auto-resolve fallback in _resolve_project_overlay_path (Stage 2.3), which walks up cwd and produces the BlarAI overlay when the cwd is BlarAI. Defaults preserved for Item 2.4 scope.
Soft inconsistency observed: iteration-2 part_b_work_plan part_b_work_plan ent n=7 (the legacy file) is enumerated in the canonical 12, with note "ALSO touched by item 2.6 -- consistent treatment per Guide-#4". The v3 handoff defers the load_config call to 2.6 treatment. Defensible interpretation: "consistent treatment" = joint disposition at 2.6 (folded chokepoint absorbs the load_config call site), not separate refactor at 2.4. This reading is acceptable BUT successor EA-4 must ensure the 2.6 work actually disposes of the load_config call (either by folding the file entirely or by threading ctx through the surviving call site). Failing to do so leaves a defaults-preservation hazard post-Stage-4 cutover when the script is invoked from devplatform cwd.
check.successor_action_required: EA-4 must verify the 2.6 disposition closes the legacy file's load_config call site before Item 2.9 single-commit.

check.ea_claim: Test baseline must hold at >= 280 passed (no regression).
check.independent_verification: `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tools/ --tb=short -q` -> "280 passed in 2.56s" EXACT match to v0e baseline (280) from EA-iter-2 pickup verification. Zero regression.

decision.ea_choice: Keep module-level REPO_ROOT constant AND add repo_root kwarg defaulting to REPO_ROOT.
decision.guide_assessment: Approved. Verified on disk that dashboard_maintainer.py:53-56 imports _git_log_oneline_since from daily_digest and invokes it without args -- this is the in-process caller D1 was protecting against. Removing REPO_ROOT would break that import and is a strictly larger semantic change than 2.4's scope authorizes. The kwarg-default pattern is risk-free, additive, and forward-compat for ctx-based per-call override.
decision.forward_direction: EA-4 should use the same pattern uniformly for any remaining files (7-13) that have analogous module-level REPO_ROOT constants AND import-side callers. Files without such callers may use ctx-only signatures (cleaner.py is a candidate per the v3 handoff).

decision.ea_choice: Helpers calling load_config receive `*, ctx: ProjectContext | None = None`; helpers using only REPO_ROOT receive `repo_root: Path = REPO_ROOT`.
decision.guide_assessment: Approved as the canonical helper-handling rule for files 7-13. Two distinct concerns (config loading vs filesystem path) deserve two distinct kwargs. Forcing single-kwarg uniformity would either over-load ctx into git-only helpers (un-needed coupling) or leak repo_root into config-only helpers (un-needed plumbing). The two-kwarg discipline is minimal-at-each-call-site.

decision.ea_choice: NOT addressed in 2.4 -- flagged for item 2.6 disposition (live._request("POST",...) on private API at line 113).
decision.guide_assessment: Approved. Verified the abuse exists at dashboard_maintainer.py:113-117. 2.4 scope is config-resolution refactor (argparse + ProjectContext threading); client-API cleanup is the chokepoint-routing concern owned by 2.6. Doing it in 2.4 would expand scope without completing the chokepoint design. Deferral is correct.
decision.forward_direction: EA-4's 2.6 disposition list MUST include dashboard_maintainer.py:113 in addition to the legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py disposition. Surface as a 2.6 work item.

decision.topic: Mid-stage handoff at 6 of 13 vs pushing into MCP server
decision.ea_choice: Emitted v3 handoff at 6 of 13 rather than pushing into self_check + blarai_next_task_resolver + cleaner.
decision.guide_assessment: Approved. Per i1-4 binding the safety valve is mid-stage handoff with docs-only commit. File 13 (vikunja_mcp/server.py, 19 handlers + project_id required-kwarg discipline) is the largest single-file lift in 2.4 and the v3 handoff explicitly warns "may consume an entire successor session by itself." Pushing further with EA-3 risked emergency-stop mid-MCP-refactor with either partial-commit damage or context-loss damage. The handoff was emitted with canonical pattern PROVEN across 6 files of varying complexity (trivial through complex/REPO_ROOT/helper-ctx) which is exactly what the successor needs as a working template.
decision.forward_direction: EA-4 should plan to take MCP server (file 13) in its own focused session, possibly emitting v4 handoff before items 2.7-2.10 if context tightens. The same i1-4 discipline applies.

required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea.intro: EA-3 has emitted and is closing this session. The acknowledgments below are addressed to the successor EA-4 (or whoever picks up the v3 handoff). They are NOT corrections to EA-3 and require no further action by EA-3. EA-4 will restate them verbatim in its iteration-1 comprehension gate.

ack.topic: Pickup verification expectations updated for LA-side housekeeping

required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea.ack: The v3 handoff's predicted ground state (10 M + 4 ?? Temp + 4 ?? new tools artifacts) does NOT include the LA-side housekeeping that landed AFTER EA-3 emitted: (a) modified docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_PROMPT.xml (+19 lines, v2.1 file-output-discipline carve-out); (b) deleted "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.10 blocked..." and "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.7 re-verify..." (LA's old-XML housekeeping); (c) renamed directory "Temp for responses" -> "temp_for_responses" (lowercase, underscores per v2.1 convention) -- visible in git as one new untracked entry "?? temp_for_responses/"; (d) two new ?? entries for EA-3's own report files (EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_GUIDE.xml + EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_LA.md). All FOUR additions are EXPECTED and not violations. EA-4's iteration-1 V0 pickup MUST account for these or it will report false drift.

ack.topic: File 12 path-label mismatch in EA reports vs v3 handoff

required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea.ack: The EA's Guide-facing completion report (.xml) and LA-facing report (.md) both label file 12 as `tools/_vikunja_client.py` (the new chokepoint, Stage 2.1). The CORRECT path for the legacy load_config caller is `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py`. The v3 handoff XML <files_pending> correctly uses `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py`, so EA-4 (which reads the v3 handoff, not the LA/Guide reports) is not impacted -- the path is correct in the source-of-truth document. Cosmetic only, but EA-4 should note the discrepancy if cross-referencing the reports.

v3 handoff plan for `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py` says "VERIFY by reading the file: confirm no module-level REPO_ROOT, no hardcoded BlarAI paths, no direct load_config calls." Independent grep shows line 180 contains `cfg = config if config is not None else config_loader.load_config()` -- a fallback load_config call. EA-4 must disposition this fallback (either remove if config is always injected by callers, or thread ctx through to make the fallback project-aware). Today's production caller (run_live.py:58) always passes config so the fallback is dead path, but it is a load_config-callsite that should be addressed in 2.4 scope.

ack.topic: Legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py disposition to 2.6 must close load_config callsite

required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea.ack: v3 handoff defers `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` to Item 2.6 ("HTTP client has no project knowledge"). Independent grep confirms line 28 contains `cfg = config_loader.load_config()` (zero-arg). The defaults-preservation chain holds today via _resolve_project_overlay_path auto-resolve from BlarAI cwd. POST-Stage-4 cutover risk: if the script is invoked from devplatform cwd, the auto-resolve resolves to a non-BlarAI ctx and the overlay merge produces wrong allowlist. EA-4 MUST ensure the Item 2.6 disposition (whether "fold entirely" or "thin shim through new chokepoint") explicitly closes the line-28 load_config callsite before Item 2.9 single-commit. If 2.6 ends up leaving the load_config call in place without ctx threading, that is a w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide.

standing_reminders_for_next_ea_session.reminder: Single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; pyproject.toml MUST be in that commit; no partial commits before 2.9.
Fleet stays paused throughout (verify state.json on each substantive edit; NEVER call state.resume_fleet -- function name reminder, AttributeError otherwise).
No merge to main during Stage 2; chore/platform-extraction is the only working surface.
stash@{0} (wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002) is UNTOUCHED -- never git stash pop / git stash apply / git stash drop.
STATUS.md updates at item 2.10 are append-only; do not edit prior Stage entries.

If context exhaustion threatens before item 2.9, emit a v4 mid-stage handoff XML following v1/v2/v3 schema (docs-only commit), then stop -- no partial-commit refactor and no emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.
$BLARAI_PID preflight at item 2.8 MUST resolve to exactly 3; empty or non-3 = STOP and report (no silent regex/yaml adaptation, per ack g4-5).

critical_notify.ps1 inspection rule (per Guide-#4 ack g4-1): default disposition is no-action with WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1 citation; runtime-path discovery = STOP and report. Same rule for any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope (tools/scheduled-tasks/, tools/fleet_ops/, tools/fleet_observability/) surfaces beyond the canonical 7.

Risk label hygiene: compound labels with reference prefixes (e.g., "R12-PS1-coverage-watch", "w3-blarai-pid-preflight-watch") are acceptable when the anchor field disambiguates them; bare numeric labels ("V1", "R12", "C3") without descriptive suffix are the violation pattern.
All 23 binding acknowledgments from EA-3's iteration-2 gate (16 pre-existing + 7 Guide-#4) plus the 4 g5-N acknowledgments above remain in force through Stage 4 cutover (per i1-2 dedup rule).

closing_handoff_recommendations_for_next.step: Run the V0a-V0g pickup verification block at session start, expecting the additional LA-side housekeeping entries per ack g5-1.
Read the three artifacts EA-3 left on disk: v3 handoff XML, Guide-facing completion report, LA-facing completion report (the latter two for context only -- v3 handoff is the authoritative work plan).
Read the 6 already-refactored files end-to-end to internalize the proven pattern before applying to files 7-13.

Refactor the 7 remaining Item-2.4 files in v3-handoff order: self_check.py -> blarai_next_task_resolver.py -> cleaner.py -> diff_builder.py -> active_tasks.py -> fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py -> vikunja_mcp/server.py. Note the file-9 + file-12 dispositions per acks g5-3 + g5-4.
If MCP server (file 13) approaches context exhaustion, emit v4 handoff per i1-4. Same single-commit discipline.
After all 13 entrypoint-side files complete + compile clean: proceed to 2.4.v2 verification, then items 2.5 through 2.10.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: APPROVED -- EA-3's 6-of-13 partial work and v3 mid-stage handoff are RATIFIED. Single-commit discipline holds. Decisions D1-D4 RATIFIED. The 4 g5-N acknowledgments are MED/LOW heads-up items addressed to the successor EA-4 (NOT corrections requiring EA-3 to re-emit). EA-3 may close this session. The Lead Architect may forward this file to the next EA chat at the next session bootstrap, alongside the standard attachment set (v1+v2+v3 handoffs, iteration-1+iteration-2 Guide reviews, EA iteration-2 gate, completion reports, Stage 2 XML, EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml, STATUS.md). No further Guide re-review required for the 4 g5-N acks; they are for audit-trail purposes and EA-4 will restate them in its iteration-1 gate.
